key_points: The story is about Margie and Tommy, two children in the future.
Margie discovers an old printed book in her attic.
The story contrasts future mechanical education with traditional schools.
Highlights the value of human interaction in learning.
Shows curiosity, imagination, and the enjoyment of learning from books.
examples: Margie’s surprise at seeing printed text in a real book.
Tommy comparing the old books with his mechanical teacher.
The story mentioning children learning from a machine rather than a human teacher.
exam_tips: Remember main characters: Margie and Tommy.
Focus on the theme: differences between past and future education.
Understand the emotions of Margie discovering the book.
Note the author’s message about the importance of learning with interaction.
